BrandRank.AI — Platform Showcase

Sample of Recent
BrandRank Dashboard Features

Brand Health and Trust Meter

01 —
Brand Health and Trust Meter

Brand new feature that sits on the home page of the dashboard, giving executives an instant composite read on brand health across all six AI engines. For the American Medical Association, it surfaces an Overall Score (7.4), a Brand Health & Trust rating (98%), and sub-scores for Visibility in AI Search (7.3) and Content Readiness (7.8) — all updated daily and designed to flag emerging risks before they compound.

02 —
AI Search Visibility — Strengths, Gaps & Competitive Positioning

Maps three dimensions in one view: where the brand wins in AI answers, where it's missing from high-intent queries, and how it stacks up against competitors. For Nescafé (Nestlé Canada), scoring 6.1, the platform flags Starbucks as a competitive threat in espresso concentrate results and surfaces specific content recommendations to close each gap.

03 —
Search Prompts by Category — Walmart at 187 Daily Phrases

Customers can now organize prompts into named categories — like "Customer Service & Support" or "Regional & Global Presence" — making it easy to track performance by business priority. Replacing or editing individual prompts is equally simple, giving teams full control over what gets measured. For Walmart, 187 phrases run daily across six engines, with color-coded scores revealing sharp engine variance: Anthropic rates Customer Service at 1.4 while Grok scores it 2.1.

04 —
Brand Promise Alignment — Does AI Reflect the Brand's Own Claims?

Asks whether AI engines actually reflect a brand's stated identity. For Amazon ("Earth's most customer-centric company"), MetaAI scores alignment at 8.2 and Grok at 8.4 — but Anthropic scores just 4.5 and Gemini 5.0. Each engine card surfaces a "Take Action" pathway so teams can respond with targeted content.

05 —
Accuracy Scoring — Hallucination Detection at Statement Level

Directly addresses the hallucination problem Gartner flagged as mandatory for this category. For Amazon, 10 brand statements are tested daily — 9 confirmed correct, one flagged false (the "$1 billion to nonprofits" claim), one amber. Each result is categorized by topic, giving communications teams a full audit trail.

06 —
AI-Powered SWOT — Real-Time Competitive Intelligence

A living SWOT built from what AI engines collectively "know" about a brand right now. For Amazon (updated March 30, 2026), it captures the Prime flywheel as a core strength, complexity as the primary weakness, and Ring's collapsed Flock Safety partnership as an emergent threat. Refreshes automatically as narratives shift.

07 —
Vulnerability Categories — Where AI Can Hurt Your Brand

Scores a brand across ten dimensions — Safety, Transparency, Sustainability, and more — benchmarked against key competitors. Amazon leads in Innovation (8.8) but trails in Transparency (5.2) and Safety (5.8), where Costco scores 7.0 and 8.2 respectively. Color-coding instantly surfaces the exposure zones that need action.

08 —
Source Authority Mapping — Who's Shaping the AI Answers?

Surfaces which publishers and websites are driving AI rankings for any given prompt. For "Where to shop with 24/7 support?" on MetaAI, Business Insider (High authority, Independent) is the key ranking driver. Competing brands like AnswerConnect and T-Mobile are also pushing their own pages into results — revealing exactly where third-party coverage investments pay off.

09 —
Competitor Prompt Analysis — Where You Rank vs. the Competition

Shows exact rankings versus named competitors for any query, on any engine. Amazon scores 8.2 but ranks #4 for "Where to shop with 24/7 support?" on MetaAI — behind Zappos, Apple, and Diapers.com. Auto-generated High Priority flags direct the response: in this case, Business Insider coverage and dedicated 24/7 support content.
